title: Occupied Space
excerpt: An occupied space is a space currently filled by a creature, object, or other effect that is being treated as present there for movement or placement resolution.
markdown:
# Occupied Space

An occupied space is a space the current game state treats as already filled by something present there. The occupying thing can be a creature, an object, or another effect whose rules make that space unavailable for ordinary placement or movement.

## Space State

- A space counts as occupied when something is currently resolved as being in it
- Occupation matters because many rules check whether a creature can move through a space, end movement there, appear there, or place an effect there
- A space can be occupied even when the occupying thing is not acting on its own, as long as the world still treats it as present

## Rules Use

### Movement And Placement
- A rule can forbid ending movement in an occupied space unless another rule explicitly allows it
- Teleportation, summoning, forced movement, and effect placement often require a destination that is not already occupied
- Large creatures or objects can occupy multiple spaces, making all of those spaces occupied while that presence remains there

### Spatial Resolution
- An occupied space can still be inside an [[area_of_effect|area of effect]]
- A space being occupied does not automatically block every effect; it mainly changes whether something else can be placed into that same space and how movement resolves around it
- Whether a rule can pass through, overlap, ignore, or displace an occupied space depends on that rule's own text

### Occupied By Objects As Well As Creatures
- A creature is not the only thing that can occupy space
- A crate, wall segment, vehicle, door, summoned barrier, or other [[object|object]] can also make a space occupied if it is presently resolved there

## Common Uses

- Choosing whether a creature can move into or stop in a space
- Determining whether a summoned creature or created effect has a valid destination
- Checking whether a teleport destination is available
- Resolving contested battlefield space around creatures, obstacles, and environmental features
